Petition For Determination Of Entitlement To Estate Distribution (Probate Code Section 11700)
Off Calendar Mar-29-2016 Continued To May-24-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Loren Lopin for Mary Mauldin. Thomas Leroux for (objector) Freddie Lloyd.

The Court asked counsel where they stand and if they are ready to set this matter for mediation/trial. Mr. Lopin stated that he does not need any further discovery, that he will need 6 hours to put on the case and that they would like an opportunity to try to resolve the matter first. Mr. Leroux stated that he learned in deposition that his clients wife may have a picture to demonstrate time spent with the son, that he was surprised at the contents of the deposition and that they are trying to contact the brother to see if he ever saw them together. The Court stated that there has been lots of time to do that and that the expectation was that discovery would be complete. The Court further stated that to the extent that Ms. Mauldin is planning to hire an attorney regarding her separate interests the estate should not be expected to pay for it and that the notice of proposed action states that she plans to hire separate litigation counsel. Mr. Lopin stated that they would expect the estate to pay in her capacity as administrator. The Court stated that they need to be careful and that it would be surprised if it is appropriate to charge the entire amount to the estate. Mr. Lopin requested that they be sent to mediation and stated that he had hoped to have met with counsel prior to this hearing. Mr. Leroux stated that they could be ready for mediation within a few weeks. The Parties agreed to participate in mediation. The Court signed the order referring the matter to Pro Bono Mediation. Mediation must be completed no later than May 10, 2016. The Parties are to notify the Probate Examiner as to the outcome of mediation by May 17, 2016. The petition for determination of entitlement to estate distribution was continued to and the Parties are to return on May 24, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of mediation. The parties were instructed to let the court know if the matter is resolved and that if the matter is not resolved it expects the parties to have met and conferred and have an agreement as to how to proceed.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Barrientos. (Not Reported)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Filing Proof Of Additional Bond.
Off Calendar Jan-25-2016 Off Calendar

Petition For Determination Of Entitlement To Estate Distribution (Probate Code Section 11700)
Off Calendar Jan-20-2016 Continued To Mar-29-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Loren Lopin for (petitioner) Mary Mauldin. Thomas LeRoux for Freddie Lloyd.

The Court stated that there is a procedural issue in that a bond has been filed in the amount of $324,000.00 and the order was for a bond in the amount of $325,000.00. Mr. Lopin stated that an additional bond in the amount of $1,000.00 was filed yesterday. The Court asked if the parties have talked to each other regarding how to proceed. Mr. LeRoux stated that Mr. Lopin would like to depose his client, that his client has agreed and that they are looking for a location in New York for his client to appear via videoconference. The Court asked if they were willing to try mediation. Mr. Lopin stated that they are not willing now, that they want to do discovery and that they want an evidentiary hearing. The Court requested that they do their discovery first then schedule the evidentiary hearing and stated that it wants to know if they are going to mediate. Mr. Lopin stated that they need to do discovery first. The Court asked other than the deposition, what else needs to be done. Mr. LeRoux stated that he may want to depose Mr. Lopin's client. Mr. Lopin stated that there are two people who have signed affidavits and that he will want to depose them. The petition for determination of entitlement to estate distribution was continued to March 29, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 with the expectation that discovery will have been completed and that the parties are prepared for mediation or to set an evidentiary hearing.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Rodrin. (Not Reported)
